_readme: THE CONTROL PANEL for your Daily Brief. Everything the morning routine does is driven by this file. The `modules` below were rebuilt from evidence in your own signals (writing, Spotify, reading, calendar, Drive) to reflect your ACTUAL interests rather than your job. Edit freely: (1) `modules` — the domains scanned, and the exact `queries`/`sources`/`lenses` behind each; (2) `layout.sections` — what appears and in what order; (3) `summer.deepDives` — this season's exploration arcs; (4) `delivery` — where the finished brief is sent. See brief/README.md and brief/summer-2026.md.

owner.name: Anthony M. Carrella
owner.voice: Literate, contemplative, unhurried, anti-ironic — total sincerity in forms that usually invite irony. A maker who builds small purpose-built instruments to pay a specific quality of attention; 'attention itself is the instrument' (Westminster Choir College, conductor). Lives on the seams between traditions — the drawn and the computed, Uyghur and Soviet, Presbyterian and Orthodox and Jungian, the classical-antique surface over the Central-Asian subject. Keeps records as an inheritance. Resists the preset box: naming should fall out of measurement, never the reverse. Prefers one thing seen well and sat with over ten skimmed.

schedule._note: Cron is in the routine's timezone. This is the wake time for the generation session. To change the time, edit this AND update the routine (see README → Scheduling).

format.title: The Daily Brief
format.subtitle: for Anthony — {longdate}
format.cadenceRule: Run every `daily` module each morning. Run a `weekly` module only on its `weekday`, so the daily read stays short — one slower field at a time.
format._note: maxItemsPerModule caps items per module (kept low on purpose — depth over volume). recencyWindowHours defines 'news'; older items are kept only if evergreen.

modules.label: Instruments of Attention
queries: small purpose-built software tools for thinking, tools for thought
Claude artifacts and Claude Code personal projects, building without writing code
parametric and generative design, custom notation systems, homemade measuring instruments
indie web personal site craft, digital gardens, hand-built microsites
AI as collaborator not optimizer, human-in-the-loop making, conductor metaphor for AI
local-first software, single-purpose apps, the joy of building your own tools

sources.detail: tools-for-thought and indie-maker writing — filtered hard for craft and idea, not product launches or hype
maker/tools-for-thought essayists and the indie web

lenses: One small, hand-built tool or artifact someone made to pay a specific kind of attention
One idea about making — the drawn vs. the computed, the score vs. the sounding, the instrument as the real medium
One thing worth trying in Claude Code or on his own site this week
modules.agentBrief: You scout the world of small, purpose-built instruments for Anthony, who builds tools the way a conductor holds a score — he keeps the intent, the machine does the sounding, and he does not write the code by hand. He dates his own history to his first Claude artifact; every one of his interests routes through a machine he made for it (a soup scorer, a naturalist's log, this brief). Bring him people making tiny single-purpose tools with total sincerity: custom notation, spectra over scores, hand-built microsites, generative design. He has a standing moral objection to AI-as-optimizer (the 'cybernetic podium') and to the reductive single number — favor makers who resist the preset box. No startup churn, no productivity-porn. Craft and idea only.

modules.label: The Silk Road Table
queries: lagman noodle history, Uyghur laghman, Central Asian hand-pulled noodles
Bukharian Jewish cuisine Rego Park Forest Hills Queens, Regoistan
Uyghur diaspora food and migration, Silk Road culinary anthropology
Soviet-era Central Asian foodways, Dungan and Uzbek cooking
Central Asian restaurants Northeast Philadelphia Bustleton Avenue, Uzbek Philly
food as anthropology, diaspora homesickness and cooking, one dish many histories

sources.detail: food anthropology, diaspora and migration writing, regional restaurant reportage in the Uyghur/Bukharian/Central Asian corridors

lenses: One thread on lagman or its cousins — a word wearing three histories (Uyghur, Bukharian, Soviet-Russian)
One doorway into diaspora, migration, or homesickness through a table
One place, corridor, or archive worth driving to when winter returns
modules.agentBrief: You keep the archive of the Silk Road table for Anthony, who spent a winter driving a noodle corridor alone at night and built a scoring instrument for lagman before realizing he'd found not a soup but an axis — one word wearing at least three histories. He knows the food is winter food and a doorway into migration and homesickness, not a foodie ranking; he abandoned the ranking premise mid-project and let it dissolve into metaphysics. Feed the anthropology, not the listicle: the Uyghur/Bukharian/Soviet seam, the real corridors (Bustleton Ave, Rego Park's 'Regoistan'), the histories that ride inside a single dish. This is summer, so lean toward reading and mapping — the eating resumes in the cold months.

modules.label: The Contemplative Hours
queries: Richard Rohr daily meditation Center for Action and Contemplation
centering prayer and Christian contemplative practice, Contemplative Outreach Shalem
Abbey of the Arts monk manifesto, contemplative retreat and silence
Orthodox spirituality and the Psalms, St Vladimir's Seminary
Karl Barth theology, Barth Center, Andrew Root ministry in a secular age
Jungian depth psychology and spirituality, gratitude as practice

sources.detail: contemplative-Christian, ecumenical, and depth-psychology writing across traditions

lenses: One meditation or line to sit with in the morning silence
One provocation from beyond the Reformed home — Catholic-contemplative, Orthodox, or Jungian
One practice, retreat, or feast-day worth marking this week
modules.agentBrief: You tend Anthony's own contemplative life — not his church job, but the interior discipline he keeps unbidden: daily Rohr meditations, Wednesday centering prayer, silent retreats, a 205-entry gratitude journal that names God 'the Idea Giver, the Critical Pedagogue.' He is ecumenically restless — Reformed at home but reading Orthodox Psalms, Barth, Abbey of the Arts, Jungian depth work, tracking Jewish and Episcopal calendars alongside his own. Bring him one thing to sit with, always substantive and anti-sentimental. He wants depth and silence, never devotional pablum or culture-war religion. When you cite scripture or a feast, name it precisely.

modules.label: Deep Listening & the Choral Ear
queries: Bremer/McCoy Danish ambient jazz duo new release
acoustic piano trio jazz e.s.t. GoGo Penguin, Dave Brubeck deep cuts
Steve Reich minimalism and process music, American minimalist composers
Yunchan Lim classical piano, contemporary pianists
Sufjan Stevens Adrianne Lenker literary confessional folk
Princeton University Chapel organ recital, sacred choral music, Berlin Philharmonic Digital Concert Hall

sources.detail: New releases and themed listens on his continuum — Bremer/McCoy, piano trio, Reich, Yunchan Lim, Sufjan, Lenker. Pair classical and jazz as one field the way he does.
sacred-music and recital calendars (Princeton Chapel Thursday organ recitals), ambient/jazz and new-classical press

lenses: One release or recording on his continuum — piano at the center, jazz trio to minimalism to sacred folk
One live sacred-music occasion — a Thursday organ recital, a choral evensong, a Digital Concert Hall stream
One note on the ear itself — conducting, deep listening, attention as the instrument
modules.agentBrief: You curate one coherent contemplative sound-field for Anthony, a Westminster Choir College-trained conductor (his lineage: Helen Kemp, James Jordan) for whom 'attention itself became the instrument.' His #1 all-time artist is the niche Danish ambient-jazz duo Bremer/McCoy — a deep-listener's tell, not a chart profile. Hold classical and jazz on one continuum (the engine natively pairs 'Yunchan Lim & Bremer/McCoy'): acoustic piano trio, Brubeck deep cuts like 'Strange Meadow Lark,' Steve Reich's process music as the structural cousin, Sufjan and Adrianne Lenker as the literary lyric voice. He keeps a live sacred-music habit — Thursday organ recitals at Princeton Chapel nearly year-round, Berlin Phil membership, OPC singing. One recording heard well beats ten names dropped.

modules.label: The Reading Room
queries: ballet criticism Marina Harss, ABT NYCB Balanchine Baryshnikov new reviews
Jungian depth psychology This Jungian Life, ARAS archetypal symbolism
integral and developmental philosophy Steve McIntosh, transhumanism and the human person
AI and spirituality Sean Tobin The Inner Exodus, technology and the soul
New York Times Book Review notable essays, literary criticism this week
documenting as stewardship, archives and record-keeping as inheritance

sources.detail: dance criticism, depth-psychology, theology, and literary essays — the systematically-organized reading life he routes into plus-aliases (+jung, +wine, +incense)

lenses: One essay or piece of criticism worth reading in full — theology, depth psychology, or the literary
One dispatch from the dance world (Marina Harss on ABT, NYCB, Balanchine)
One idea about archive, record-keeping, or the human person to keep and highlight
modules.agentBrief: You feed Anthony's reading life, which he runs as a system — voracious, cross-domain, routed into purpose-built inbox aliases (+jung, +wine, +incense) and kept in Readwise because 'documenting is stewardship, and a record kept is an inheritance made.' His beats: serious Barth/academic theology, Jungian depth psychology (ARAS, This Jungian Life), a genuine ballet-criticism habit (Marina Harss), integral/developmental philosophy (McIntosh), psychology-meets-AI (Sean Tobin), the NYT Book Review. His mind makes wide, fast, cross-domain sweeps it can't hold; you are part of the net that catches them. Bring him one thing worth reading in full and keeping — not summaries of summaries. Total sincerity, no irony.

modules.label: The Antiquity Cosmos
queries: Greek black-figure vase painting, amphorae and Attic pottery, terracotta
Aegean and Greek key meander ornament, ancient Mediterranean design
display typeface and letterform design, bespoke fonts and type specimens
invented notation systems and constructed scripts, taxonomy as world-building
Etruscan art and material culture, Greco-Aegean visual world
metallic gold and copper leaf, papyrus and antique surface texture in design

sources.detail: American art that touches antiquity, letterform, and taxonomy — works and artists to contemplate. Preserve artist attribution and the whitney.org link.
classical archaeology, type-design, and ornament/notation writing

lenses: One object from the antique Mediterranean world — a vase, a meander, an amphora — seen closely
One piece of letterform or notation craft — a display face, a specimen, an invented grammar
One idea about taxonomy or world-building — naming that falls out of measurement, never the reverse
modules.agentBrief: You feed Anthony's fully-realized Greco-Aegean visual world — not decoration but a cosmos: four hand-labeled modes (Dark/Light/Etruscan/Aegean), four bespoke display faces (Aegean, Spartacus, Xiphos, Columns), meanders, black-figure terracotta, torn papyrus, gold-with-copper-and-green metallic sheen. He delights in inventing rigorous-looking notation for its own sake (mock molecular sigils like Lg₁₀ and Br₀₆) — taxonomy and letterform as world-building, executed with total sincerity. Bring him ancient objects to look at closely, type and notation craft, and anything where naming falls out of measurement rather than the reverse. Use the Whitney MCP where American art touches these themes, always keeping attribution and the link.

modules.label: Field Notes & the Ritual of the Senses
queries: backyard birding and phenology, catbird white-throated sparrow behavior, seasonal observation
faith and ecology, Waterspirit, contemplative naturalism, more-than-human world
New Jersey Pennington-area natural history, whitetail deer and native plants
third-wave coffee roaster Char and Stave Ardmore, single-origin coffee ritual
Japanese incense Kikoh, ceremonial matcha Ooika, De Soi non-alcoholic aperitif
Burgundy value wine picks, natural wine, small sensory ritual and attention

sources.detail: contemplative natural-history and phenology writing, faith-and-ecology, and the curated sensory beats (specialty coffee, matcha, incense, non-alc, Burgundy)

lenses: One field note worth carrying into the walk — a bird, a bloom, a turn of the season
One thread of faith-and-ecology or contemplative naturalism
One small sensory ritual — a coffee, an incense, a matcha, a bottle — chosen with care
modules.agentBrief: You keep Anthony's devotional attention to the more-than-human world and his booze-optional ritual of the senses — of a piece with each other and with his prayer. He runs a 49-record Natural Observations journal piped live into his art, bows to whitetail deer, rehomes yellowjackets rather than killing them, notices catbird families and non-native grass rhizomes, and reads Waterspirit's faith-and-ecology. Alongside: a Sunday coffee pickup at Char and Stave treated as a fixed appointment, Kikoh Japanese incense, Ooika matcha, De Soi non-alc, SuperVinoBros Burgundy value picks — each curated with the same care as his spiritual reading. Bring seasonal field notes and one well-chosen sensory pleasure. Contemplative, unhurried, never gear-review noise.

personalContext._note: Pulls from your own connected accounts to open the brief with 'your actual day'. Requires the relevant connectors; each is optional and degrades gracefully if offline.

calendar.detail: Today's events (and anything notable tomorrow) from your personal calendar. Summarize as a clean agenda. Note contemplative/retreat blocks and the Thursday Princeton organ recital when they fall.

inbox.detail: Only threads that genuinely need attention today. Never quote sensitive or private-relationship content in the published web artifact; keep specifics to the private delivery channels.

layout._note: The brief is assembled from these sections IN THIS ORDER. Each `kind` maps to a renderer in static/brief/js/app.js. `module` entries name a module via `from`. Weekly modules only appear on their `weekday`. Reorder/remove/add freely; to invent a new kind, add a renderer of the same name.

sections.title: Field Notes & the Ritual of the Senses
For the Radar
sections._note: Slower-horizon items the scanners flagged as worth watching but not acting on today — including the summer deep-dive tracks.

summer._note: You want to spend this summer DISCOVERING these interests, so the brief is also an arc, not just a feed. Each track is a 6-10 week exploration with concrete first moves. The routine surfaces the current track's next move in the brief's 'For the Radar' section. Full write-up: brief/summer-2026.md.

deepDives.title: The Silk Road Table, read before it is eaten
deepDives.why: Lagman is winter food — his own tracking made its seasonality legible to him before he'd consciously registered it. So summer is not for eating the corridor but for reading it: turning the abandoned ranking project into real anthropology, so that when the cold returns he drives it with three histories in his mouth instead of a score sheet. This is the season to let the instrument finish dissolving into metaphysics.
deepDives.arc: Weeks 1-2: the Uyghur laghman lineage and the Turkic noodle family. Weeks 3-4: the Bukharian Jewish table and Rego Park's 'Regoistan' — how the same word crossed into a different diaspora. Weeks 5-6: the Soviet-Russian inheritance and Dungan/Uzbek variants — one word, three histories, fully mapped. Weeks 7-8: re-map the real corridors (Bustleton Ave, Forest Hills) and design the winter's eating as fieldwork, not ranking. Weeks 9-10: write the essay the Log was always reaching for — 'what my people ate at 2 AM and why' — and let the archive stand as an inheritance.
firstMoves: Pull every histories-in-one-word thread and start a Thymer collection: 'lagman — three histories,' one card per tradition (Uyghur / Bukharian / Soviet-Russian)
Read one serious piece on Bukharian Jewish foodways in Queens and one on Uyghur diaspora cooking; highlight into Readwise
Re-drive nothing — instead map the Bustleton Ave and Rego Park corridors on paper, marking which restaurant belongs to which history
Write 500 words answering his own line: 'I hadn't discovered a soup. I'd discovered an axis.' What is the axis?

deepDives.title: The whole continuum: from Bremer/McCoy outward
deepDives.why: His deepest listening lives on one field where classical and jazz are not separate genres but a single continuum — the engine already pairs 'Yunchan Lim & Bremer/McCoy.' A conductor who was trained ear-first has never mapped his own sound-world as a whole. This summer he draws the map, and lets the choral ear that formed him become audible in the ambient-jazz and minimalism he loves.
deepDives.arc: Weeks 1-2: sit inside Bremer/McCoy's full catalog and name what the ear is doing. Weeks 3-4: the acoustic piano trio lineage — e.s.t., GoGo Penguin, Brubeck deep cuts past 'Take Five.' Weeks 5-6: Steve Reich and American minimalism as the structural cousin — process as form. Weeks 7-8: Yunchan Lim and the classical piano end of the continuum, heard as one field with the jazz. Weeks 9-10: the literary lyric voice (Sufjan, Adrianne Lenker) and a built artifact — a sequenced playlist or a listening-portal that holds the whole continuum in order.
firstMoves: Open the Spotify MCP and build a private playlist seed 'The Continuum,' starting with the Bremer/McCoy track that returns first for him
Attend or stream one Thursday 12:30 organ recital at Princeton Chapel and write three sentences on what the ear noticed
Listen through Brubeck's 'Strange Meadow Lark' and one full Reich piece back to back; note what they share
Draft the one-line thesis of the continuum — how Westminster's 'attention itself became the instrument' shows up in music he didn't make

deepDives.title: A summer of the interior — Psalms, Barth, and the deeper silence
deepDives.why: His contemplative life is genuinely his own, but it runs on daily fragments — a Rohr meditation here, a Wednesday centering-prayer hour there. His calendar already clusters a retreat season into these months (Retreat Prep → Retreat → Silent Retreat at Overbrook). Summer is the season to give the ecumenical restlessness a spine: one sustained text, read slowly, across traditions, instead of a scattered feed.
deepDives.arc: Weeks 1-2: the Psalms as a whole, guided by the St Vladimir's Orthodox lens — pray one a day. Weeks 3-4: bring Barth and Andrew Root alongside — the Reformed home in conversation with the silence. Weeks 5-6: the Jungian read — the Psalms and gratitude practice as depth work, God as 'the Idea Giver, the Critical Pedagogue.' Weeks 7-8: the clustered retreat / silent-retreat window — carry the whole summer's reading into the actual silence. Weeks 9-10: integration — write the gratitude entries the season asks for and let the practice, not the feed, be the record.
firstMoves: Choose the Psalter and a fixed morning time; pray Psalm 1 on day one
Re-open the St Vladimir's 'Exploring the Book of Psalms' material and set the reading order
Add the retreat-season blocks to the calendar now so the arc lands inside the silence, not before it
Resume the gratitude journal with one entry naming what this summer's reading is giving — in his own idiom for God

deepDives.title: Build one new instrument — the naturalist's phenology machine
deepDives.why: Every interest he has routes through a small machine he made for it, and the Log was 'the first time I used one to make.' He has a 49-record Natural Observations journal but no instrument that pays attention to it the way the Lagman Log paid attention to soup. Summer — birds nesting, seasons turning legibly — is exactly when a phenology instrument would have the most to notice. This is the track where making, field naturalism, and the antiquity-letterform delight all meet.
deepDives.arc: Weeks 1-2: watch the existing Natural Observations feed and decide what axis it wants (not a score — a spectrum, seasonality, presence). Weeks 3-4: sketch the instrument in Claude Code — feeds, hand-drawn masks, the Portal grammar. Weeks 5-6: give it a face from the antiquity cosmos — a bespoke display type, a meander frame, spectra over numbers. Weeks 7-8: wire in live seasonal data and let it start noticing catbirds, sparrows, the turn toward autumn. Weeks 9-10: let it run daily and become, like the Log, 'a footnote to' the world's own log — a record kept as an inheritance.
firstMoves: Re-read builders-mark.md and the 'bowl-rating widget' lineage passage to reconnect the making to its why
Open the Natural Observations Thymer collection and list the axes it is already implicitly tracking
Start a fresh Claude Code project and stub one Portal slot: a place, a hand-drawn mask, a live feed
Decide the one thing this instrument refuses to reduce to a single number — and name the spectrum instead

deepDives.title: The four-mode cosmos, deepened at the source
deepDives.why: His Greco-Aegean world is fully realized as surface — four modes, four bespoke faces, meanders and metallic sheen — but built largely from amphora references rather than the deep well of classical material culture. He lives on the seam of 'the classical-antique surface over the Central-Asian subject.' A summer at the source would let the world-building rest on real vase-painting, real letterforms, real taxonomy — sincerity earning its rigor.
deepDives.arc: Weeks 1-2: Greek black-figure and Attic vase painting — the grammar behind Etruscan Mode. Weeks 3-4: the meander and Aegean ornament as a system, not a motif. Weeks 5-6: type and letterform history feeding his four faces (Aegean, Spartacus, Xiphos, Columns). Weeks 7-8: invented notation and constructed scripts — the tradition his molecular sigils belong to. Weeks 9-10: revise the four modes with what he's learned and design one new artifact — a specimen sheet or a notation grammar — that could only exist after this summer.
firstMoves: Use the Whitney MCP to pull American works that touch antiquity, letterform, or taxonomy and start a reference board
Read one primer on Greek black-figure technique; note what his Etruscan Mode gets right and what it invents
Lay his four display faces beside real classical letterforms and mark the choices he'd now make differently
Sketch one new invented notation — in the spirit of Lg₁₀ and Br₀₆ — for something he's currently measuring

delivery._note: The web artifact is the canonical output and always produced. Other channels layer on top and toggle independently. `visibility: private` sections (agenda/inbox) are included in email/Slack but stripped from the public web artifact.

web._note: Writes latest.json + a dated archive file + updates index.json, then commits to main so GitHub Pages redeploys.

email._note: mode 'draft' creates a Gmail draft you review before sending; switch to 'send' once you trust it. Renders the full brief (incl. private sections) as HTML with a link to the live version.

slack._note: Posts headline + section highlights + link as a self-DM.

pdf._note: Print stylesheet makes 'Save as PDF' work today; automated PDF via headless Chromium documented in brief/exporters/README.md.

screenshots._note: Planned: headless-Chromium capture of each section. See brief/exporters/README.md.

thymer._note: Planned: export the brief as a Thymer note (you live in Thymer). Markdown drop-in now; API when a connector exists. See brief/exporters/README.md.
